bullets.text: 2000 was unacceptable: the combined ratio missed the 4% underwriting profit target by 8.4 points; ROE was 1.7% versus 10.9% in 1999; five-year average underwriting margin was 3.2% with 16% average annual growth.
Net premiums written grew only 1% in 2000 as aggressive rate increases late in 1999 and throughout 2000 made Progressive less competitive; management prioritized rate adequacy over volume growth.

Progressive returned to the 4% annual underwriting profit standard, increased rates rapidly, and shifted 80% of policies to six-month terms (from 35% semiannual in 1999) to accelerate the impact of rate changes.

Claims restructured as a separate department reporting to CEO; new claim management team of 16 with average 16 years Progressive experience overhauled file ownership, accountability, and quality calibration processes.

Loss reserves were strengthened in 2000 after years of gradual reserve releases left Progressive uncomfortably close to inadequacy; management committed to publishing a detailed reserve analysis for the first time since 1995.

Agency group expense ratio fell to 18.8% from 20.7% in 1999; Direct business expense ratio fell to 29.6% from 38.5%, aided by improved advertising efficiency, higher renewals percentage, and reduced Gainsharing payout of $8.3 million (down from $55.6 million in 1999).

Progressive earned 8.6% total portfolio return in 2000; repaid $300 million of 10% and 10.125% debt in December; repurchased 272,500 shares at average $58.09; debt-to-total capital ratio fell to 20.7%.

Internet business reached 15% of Direct NPW in 2000, up from 7% in 1999, and 3% of total company volume; progressive.com was first auto insurance site on Web-enabled cellular phones; Progressive won Gomez No. 1 online insurance carrier award for three consecutive quarters.

Peter Lewis wrote his final letter as CEO (36 years in role), with Glenn Renwick officially becoming CEO on January 1, 2001; Lewis attributed 2000's failures partly to his own organizational and tactical decisions prior to 2000.
